steps.title: Apply zero product property
steps.content:
When a product of factors equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero. For the equation $$-4(v-9)(v-2)(v+7) = 0$$ the constant $-4$ is never zero, so we set each variable factor equal to zero:
  • $v - 9 = 0$ gives $v = 9$
  • $v - 2 = 0$ gives $v = 2$
  • $v + 7 = 0$ gives $v = -7$

steps.title: Identify the solution in the choices
steps.content: The solutions are $v = 9$, $v = 2$, and $v = -7$. Of these, only $v = 9$ appears in the answer choices. That matches choice C — C is correct.
